result.finding: The application uses HTTP as its sole communication protocol. It exposes HTTP endpoints via Spring MVC (@GetMapping, @PostMapping) for server-side rendered HTML pages and one JSON REST endpoint (/vets). No gRPC, message queue, WebSocket, or TCP/UDP protocols are used.
evidence: @GetMapping and @PostMapping annotations found in OwnerController, PetController, VisitController, VetController, WelcomeController, CrashController
VetController exposes GET /vets.html and GET /vets (JSON response for application/json Accept header)
No spring-boot-starter-amqp, spring-kafka, or messaging dependencies in pom.xml
No WebSocket, SignalR, or TCP socket usage found in source code
Spring Boot Actuator exposes management endpoints over HTTP (/actuator/*)
values: Style: Server-side rendered web (Thymeleaf) + limited REST (JSON)
